user_message: Working on Wide World Importers's competitive positioning. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me prep a brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 11:19 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 14:19 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 15:41 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Wide World Importers

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-J trap (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-J failure mode (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes'))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-J -- Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')
